hero.title: The Po Bridgehead
hero.subtitle: Major David Lewis, 1st Welch Regiment — 25 April 1945

media.alt: David Lewis with campaign medals beside the Royal Welsh regimental goat and uniformed handler
media.caption: David John Lewis — university student, twenty-one, commissioned into the Welch Regiment on 23 August 1940

media.alt: British troops advance through Pachino during the Allied invasion of Sicily, 10 July 1943. IWM NA 4411
media.caption: Sicily, July 1943 — the 1st Welch joined the 168th Brigade for the invasion. Photograph: IWM NA 4411

media.alt: Wartime letter from Lieut. D.J. Lewis, 1 Welch R., Central Mediterranean Force, 16 August 1943
media.caption: Lewis's own letter from the Italian front — 'If anything should happen to me you can still claim the money'

media.alt: Wireless operator Bombardier S. Williams in a shallow dugout at Anzio, 1944. IWM NA 12374
media.caption: Anzio — the 168th Brigade suffered fifty per cent casualties in six weeks in the flat, waterlogged beachhead. Photograph: IWM NA 12374

media.alt: A 3.7-inch AA gun bombarding enemy positions on the Gothic Line, 2 September 1944. IWM NA 18197
media.caption: The Gothic Line — the 56th Division took nearly half of Eighth Army's Operation Olive casualties. Photograph: IWM NA 18197

media.alt: Royal Artillery searchlight crew cleaning the mirror on the south bank of the Po, the pontoon bridge reflected in the glass, 30 April 1945. IWM TR 2854
media.caption: The 1st Welch was rebuilt from cadre and attached to the 6th Armoured Division for the spring offensive. Photograph: IWM TR 2854

media.alt: Pontoon Bailey bridge across the River Po near Ferrara, with the wrecked original bridge behind, 30 April 1945. IWM TR 2845
media.caption: The Po bridgehead — Lewis's 1st Welch crossed the river under fire on 25 April 1945. Photograph: IWM TR 2845

media.alt: The United States Silver Star medal — gold five-pointed star on red, white and blue ribbon, on dark green cloth
media.caption: 'Major Lewis led a platoon to assault the strong German positions … and by his personal efforts and example successfully overran the enemy'

media.alt: Fulvia and David Lewis at home in later years
media.caption: Major David Lewis — Sicily to Austria, the full length of the Italian peninsula
